response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, a geo-data platform; GIS work for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second, plus Linux and distributed-systems exposure. However, his professional C++ experience is dated (2014–2015 at National Instruments), he has no Rust, and his recent roles are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than systems engineering. He has no direct routing or navigation pipeline experience, so fit for the core C++ routing-tiles work is uncertain despite strong adjacent signals.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective and GIS work for Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and Linux proficiency
concerns: Modern C++ experience is roughly a decade old and no Rust experience is listed
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline background
Recent focus (2020–present) is TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, not systems-level development
